items.headline: Bonn zieht vorübergehend Großteil der Stadtbahnen aus dem Verkehr
items.summary: In Bonn müssen vorübergehend 60 von insgesamt 75 Straßenbahnen aus dem Verkehr gezogen werden. Nach WDR-Information hatten Prüfer Schäden an Rädern und eine lange Liste mit Mängeln festgestellt. Nun müssen die Bahnen überprüft werden.

items.headline: Seeblockade: Huthi-Miliz zwingt offenbar Schiffe zur Umkehr
items.summary: Die Huthi-Miliz lässt ihrer Ankündigung einer Seeblockade gegen das verfeindete Saudi-Arabien offenbar Taten folgen. Die Gruppe aus dem Jemen hat nach eigenen Angaben mehrere Schiffe zur Umkehr gezwungen.

items.headline: Chalil al-Haja: Was die Wahl des neuen Hamas-Chefs bedeutet
items.summary: Nach einer fast zweijährigen Übergangsphase hat die Terrororganisation Hamas einen neuen Anführer. Die Wahl von Chalil al-Haja steht laut Experten für eine Richtungsentscheidung - gegen pragmatischere Strömungen. Von M. Marks.

items.headline: Marktbericht: DAX hält sich auf hohem Niveau
items.summary: Mit etwas Rückenwind aus New York hat sich der DAX leicht stabilisiert - und bleibt damit auf hohem Niveau nahe der runden Marke von 25.000 Punkten. Sogar eine positive Überraschung gab es heute.

items.headline: Türkei: Abgesetzter CHP-Chef Özel kündigt Gründung neuer Partei an
items.summary: Im Mai hatte die türkische Justiz den Oppositionspolitiker Özel als Chef der CHP-Partei abgesetzt - nun hat er die Gründung einer neuen Partei angekündigt. Sie könnte auf einen Schlag die größte Oppositionspartei im Parlament werden.

items.headline: Milliarden-Deal: Microsoft baut KI-Partnerschaft mit Mistral aus
items.summary: Der Tech-Riese Microsoft und das französische KI-Unternehmen Mistral AI wollen ihre Zusammenarbeit deutlich ausweiten. Ein milliardenschweres Abkommen soll die KI-Infrastruktur in Europa spürbar stärken.

items.headline: Bundeskartellamt verhängt Millionenbußgeld wegen Reifenpreisen
items.summary: Drei Unternehmen sollen den Verkauf von Reifen mit unzulässigen Preisvorgaben beeinflusst haben. Deshalb hat das Bundeskartellamt Bußgelder von fast zwölf Millionen Euro verhängt.

items.headline: Ein Alkoholverbot gegen die Gewalt an Bahnhöfen?
items.summary: Kein Bier, kein Sektchen und kein Kurzer - die Deutsche Bahn will Alkohol an Bahnhöfen verbieten, für mehr Sicherheit. In Köln und anderen Bahnhöfen ist das schon jetzt Standard. Was hat es dort gebracht? Von Katharina Spreier.

items.headline: Nextcloud: Wirrwarr um Website-Störung – Cyberangriff bestätigt
items.summary: Am Sonntag ging die Webseite von Nextcloud vorübergehend offline. Der Anbieter bestätigt auf Anfrage einen Cyberangriff.

items.headline: Suno-Datenleck: Have I Been Pwned ergänzt 55 Millionen Konten
items.summary: Das Have-I-Been-Pwned-Projekt hat mehr als 55 Millionen Konten aus dem Suno-Datenleck zur Datenhalde hinzugefügt.

items.headline: AliExpress: EU-Kommission verhängt DSA-Rekordstrafe
items.summary: Der chinesische Onlinemarktplatz AliExpress hat über Jahre gegen den Digital Services Act verstoßen und muss nun 550 Millionen Euro zahlen.

items.headline: Microsoft arbeitet an Lösung für WSUS-Störungen
items.summary: Die Update-Software WSUS hat seit vergangener Woche mit Problemen zu kämpfen, hat Microsoft nun eingeräumt. Es gibt erste Fixes.

items.headline: WTF: Billionenbeträge berechnet: Abrechnungsfehler bei Amazon Web Services
items.summary: Kunden wurden plötzlich astronomische Kosten für Amazon Web Services angezeigt. Dahinter steckt ein technischer Fehler.

items.headline: Moonshot AI präsentiert Kimi K3: Chinas Antwort auf OpenAI und Anthropic
items.summary: Chinas KI-Start-up Moonshot AI setzt die USA mit seinem neuen Sprachmodell unter Druck. Wie reagieren Washington und das Silicon Valley auf den Wettbewerb?

items.headline: LHS 1140 b: Erstmals Atmosphäre bei erdähnlichem Exoplaneten nachgewiesen
items.summary: Seit seiner Entdeckung gilt LHS 1140 b als besonders interessant für die Suche nach außerirdischem Leben. Nun wurde bei dem Exoplaneten Helium entdeckt.

items.headline: Google Chrome: Außerplanmäßiges zweites Update in der Woche
items.summary: Google aktualisiert Chrome eigentlich jeden Mittwoch. Diese Woche folgt ein zweites Update, das mehrere kritische Lücken schließt.

items.headline: Anzeige: Großer Balkonkraftwerks-Sale bei Amazon - und der Topseller ist schon weg
items.summary: Seit gestern befinden sich mehrere Balkonkraftwerke bei Amazon im zeitlich befristeten Angebot. Der erste Deal ist schon vegriffen. (Balkonkraftwerk, Technik/Hardware)

items.headline: Anzeige: 16,1 Zoll großer Portable Monitor mit Full-HD-Auflösung für unter 90 Euro
items.summary: Der unter Amazon-Kunden beliebte Arzopa Z1C ist im befristeten Angebot zum Tiefpreis erhältlich. Weniger als jetzt kostet er selten. (TV & Monitore, Display)

items.headline: Anzeige: Bluetooth-Kopfhörer von Anker Soundcore bei Amazon günstig wie am Prime Day
items.summary: Bei Amazon sind In-Ear-Kopfhörer von Anker Soundcore jetzt zu Preisen wie am Prime Day erhältlich. Diesmal gilt der Preis allerdings für alle. (Kopfhörer, Audio/Video)

items.headline: Zölle: Käufer wollen Geld zurück - Nintendo sagt Nein
items.summary: Einige Spieler verlangen eine Rückzahlung, falls Nintendo bereits gezahlte US-Strafzölle vom Staat zurückerhält. Das Unternehmen wehrt sich. (Nintendo, Spiele)

items.headline: Anzeige: Elektronische Parkscheibe mit Solar für nur 20,79 Euro bei Amazon
items.summary: Amazon hat eine elektronische Parkscheibe im Angebot, die die Parkzeit automatisch einstellt und dank Solarunterstützung lange läuft. (Technik/Hardware)

items.headline: Bürgerenergie: Warum Energy Sharing in Österreich funktioniert
items.summary: In Österreich teilt man Energie lokal, auf Bundesebene, zwischen Nachbarn oder via Onlineplattform. Das hat nicht nur technische Gründe. Von Mario Petzold (Strom, Solarenergie)

items.headline: Anzeige: Scheppach-Werkzeugkoffer mit 173 Teilen zum neuen Bestpreis für unter 80 Euro bei Amazon
items.summary: Der Scheppach TB173 packt 173 Werkzeuge aus Chrom-Vanadium-Stahl zum neuen Bestpreis in einen 6,3-kg-Koffer. (Technik/Hardware, Bosch)

items.headline: Anzeige: Besteller-Soundbar mit Dolby Atmos und 340 W soll nur 129,98 Euro kosten
items.summary: Bei Amazon wird derzeit die Bestseller-Soundbar mit Dolby Atmos und 340 Watt Leistungsangabe im befristeten Angebot verkauft. Zeit, mal genauer hinzuhören, was man für den Preis bekommt. (Soundbar, Amazon)

items.headline: Row escalates in Ukraine over army chief's future
items.summary: Protesters are demanding Oleksandr Syrskyi be removed and former defence minister Mykhailo Fedorov reinstated by Friday.

items.headline: Lebanese army says troops deploying in 'pilot zone' after Israeli withdrawal
items.summary: A US-brokered agreement should see Israeli troops pulling out of a small part of Lebanon occupied during their conflict with Hezbollah.

items.headline: Carney looking at 'all options' as Trump announces 50% tariffs on Canada
items.summary: The duties mark a major escalation in trade tensions between the North American neighbours.

items.headline: Two French firefighters die tackling blaze near Bordeaux airport
items.summary: The blaze close to the parking lots at Bordeaux-Mérignac Airport is said to have originated from a brush fire.

items.headline: US launches fresh strikes on Iran, as Trump warns of retaliation for deaths of soldiers
items.summary: Iran says it has hit two ships in the Strait of Hormuz as well as targets in Bahrain and Jordan.

items.headline: Brazil's Amazon sees sharp decline in size of area burned in 2025
items.summary: The amount of land burned in the world's largest rainforest was the smallest since MapBiomas records began in 1985.

items.headline: Israeli strikes in Gaza kill 12, including family of six, medics say
items.summary: Firas al-Masri, his wife and their four children died in one attack, which the Israeli military says targeted a Hamas operative.

items.headline: Watch Texas police officer pull man from burning car
items.summary: Bodycam and dashcam footage shows a police officer rescue a man seconds before his car was engulfed in flames in Texas.

items.headline: AI minister role boosted but tech department axed in Burnham shake-up
items.summary: Kanishka Narayan will attend cabinet - but no longer have the leadership of a technology secretary.

items.headline: Thousands of websites taken down for illegal World Cup streams
items.summary: More than 1,000 illegal streaming sites were shut down in the US during the tournament, with a similar amount blocked in Colombia.

items.headline: Ofcom unable to take further legal action against suicide forum
items.summary: The regulator said it had "no further legal routes available" under the Online Safety Act.

items.headline: AliExpress fined record €550m by EU for allowing sale of illegal goods
items.summary: The Chinese retailer allowed unsafe toys and counterfeit clothes to be sold on its site, says the EU.

items.headline: Tech Now
items.summary: Ammie Sekhon goes behind the scenes of WWE to see the tech that supports the show.

items.headline: China's Moonshot AI claims Kimi K3 can rival OpenAI and Anthropic
items.summary: The company has unveiled a massive new artificial intelligence model it says can take on top American firms.

items.headline: Trump Media to sell instant access to 'market-moving' social posts
items.summary: Trump Media is launching a fast, paid feed to its most influential posts for Wall Street traders.

items.headline: TikTok faces Ofcom investigation over child age checks
items.summary: It follows a review by the regulator in May that criticised the platform for not being "safe enough" for children.

items.headline: Steam Deck sales are losing steam since May's price hike
items.summary: Analysis of bestseller charts shows an 80 percent dip from 2025's sales rate.

items.headline: Google announces Gemini 3.6 Flash and cybersecurity AI, teases 3.5 Pro and Gemini 4
items.summary: There are new 3.6 and 3.5 models today, but Google is already training Gemini 4.

items.headline: TreeSize won't renew perpetual-license support unless users subscribe
items.summary: "Current economic conditions" have shifted TreeSize's business model.

items.headline: When your vehicle outlives its cloud: What happens next?
items.summary: Automakers love connected vehicles, but support isn't open-ended.

items.headline: Apps targeted at US troops contain Chinese and Russian code
items.summary: More than one-eighth of apps analyzed contained foreign code.

items.headline: 2027 Kia Seltos first drive gives you plenty of SUV for not much $
items.summary: It starts at just under $25,000, but the hybrid won't arrive until next year.

items.headline: DA: Cop covered bodycam to snap nude prisoners on his iPhone—but other cams caught him
items.summary: Pennsylvania cop charged with oppression and obstruction.

items.headline: Firefighting drones in the works as wildfires plague US nearly year-round
items.summary: California and XPRIZE competition tests whether drones can stop wildfires early.

items.headline: Data centers expected to use 4x more electricity by 2035
items.summary: New data centers built through 2033 could consume as much electricity as India uses today.

items.headline: Google releases three new Gemini models — but no 3.5 Pro
items.summary: Google released Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite, and Flash Cyber, but the continued absence of Gemini 3.5 Pro raises fresh questions about its AI strategy.

items.headline: US threatens sanctions against Chinese AI models over IP theft
items.summary: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said the U.S. could sanction Chinese open AI models over alleged IP theft, expanding the Trump administration's campaign to slow China's AI advances.

items.headline: Music streamer Deezer says more than 50% of daily uploads are AI-generated
items.summary: Deezer said more than 90,000 AI-generated tracks were uploaded daily on the platform in June.

items.headline: Gritt exits stealth with $32 million for robots to build solar plants — then, everything else
items.summary: Gritt is coming out of stealth with $34 million and plans to automate the hardest tasks on construction sites.

items.headline: Anthropic’s landmark $1.5B copyright settlement is approved
items.summary: The final approval settles one case, but it doesn't resolve the broader issue of using copyrighted works to train AI models.

items.headline: Trump’s latest AI czar has already resigned
items.summary: The director role for the Center for AI Standards and Innovation (CAISI) has become a revolving door since David Sacks left his position as czar.

items.headline: Google is working on a new AI chip designed to make Gemini more efficient
items.summary: Alphabet, Google's parent company, is reportedly working on a new chip designed to make its Gemini models run much more efficiently.

items.headline: Anthropic’s $1.5 billion book piracy settlement approved by judge
items.summary: A federal judge has signed off on Anthropic's $1.5 billion class action settlement with authors who accused the company of training its AI models on copyrighted books, as reported earlier by Reuters. In an order on Monday, Judge Araceli Martínez-Olguín writes that the settlement

items.headline: Google launches a cheaper alternative to large AI security models like Mythos
items.summary: Google is launching Gemini 3.6 Flash alongside a new security model dedicated to quickly finding and patching security vulnerabilities. In a blog post on Tuesday, Google describes Gemini 3.5 Flash Cyber as a "cost-efficient and highly capable alternative" to larger, more expensiv

items.headline: Halliday’s latest smart glasses feature a much-improved display
items.summary: I was not a fan of the original Halliday glasses. The Gen 2 are better. | Image: Halliday I first slipped on Halliday's original smart glasses at CES 2025. I was not a fan. The glasses had a tiny, movable display window embedded into the frame that was incredibly finicky to see,

items.headline: Here are the 30,000 songs Sony is suing Udio’s AI music generator over
items.summary: Sony Music Entertainment has filed another lawsuit against Udio, accusing the AI music generator of infringing the copyright of more than 30,000 of its songs, ranging from Elvis Presley's Hound Dog to Beyoncé's Say My Name, and Harry Styles' As It Was. The lawsuit, filed in a New

items.summary: AUUUUUGH | Image: Cath Virginia / The Verge, Getty Images Index funds are touted as one of the safest ways to invest. Rather than picking and choosing individual stocks, index funds let you bet on the market as a whole. So what happens when a company like SpaceX - a giant gamble,

items.headline: Adobe’s ‘natural look’ camera app embraces generative AI
items.summary: Welcome to the “what is a photo” debate, Adobe. | Image: Adobe Adobe's experimental camera app has taken an unexpected turn. After Project Indigo was launched last year to provide a "more natural (SLR-like) look" for iPhone photography, the Indigo camera app is now being updated

items.headline: China delivers a one-two punch to America’s AI dominance
items.summary: China's leading AI companies are ramping up the pressure on Silicon Valley, as Moonshot and Alibaba unveiled models they claim can go toe-to-toe with the best from OpenAI and Anthropic at a fraction of the cost. The rapid-fire releases suggest America's lead at the AI frontier is

items.headline: The AI compute gap: Enterprises are buying infrastructure faster than they can measure what it costs
items.summary: Across 107 enterprises, AI infrastructure spending is accelerating well ahead of the ability to see or steer its economics. Most organizations run their AI on a familiar base of hyperscalers and model-provider APIs, yet the next dollar is aimed at specialized compute almost none

items.headline: The agent security gap: 54% of enterprises have already had an AI agent incident, and most still let agents share credentials
items.summary: Across 107 enterprises, AI agents are being given real access to systems and data while the controls meant to contain them lag behind. More than half have already had a confirmed agent security incident or a near-miss; only about a third give every agent its own scoped identity,

items.headline: The AI context gap: Enterprise AI organizations have a trust problem, not a retrieval problem — and most are still building the fix
items.summary: Across 101 enterprises, the infrastructure that feeds AI agents their business context is being built faster than it can be trusted. Retrieval-augmented generation is already the default context source, and provider-native retrieval has quietly overtaken the dedicated vector data

items.headline: The agent evaluation gap: Enterprise AI organizations have a reality-alignment problem, not a coverage problem — and most are shipping to production anyway
items.summary: Across 157 enterprises, organizations are granting AI agents more autonomy while trusting the evaluations meant to gate that autonomy less. Half have already shipped an agent that passed their internal evaluations and then failed a customer in production; only one in twenty fully

items.headline: Agentic orchestration: Enterprise AI organizations have a deployment problem, not a platform problem — and most are calling chatbots agents
items.summary: Across 101 enterprises, agent orchestration is consolidating onto model-provider platforms — Anthropic’s Claude leads by a wide margin — chosen for the gravity of the underlying model and judged on reliable multi-step execution. But the ambition runs well ahead of the reality: mo

items.headline: Google just redesigned the search box for the first time in 25 years — here’s why it matters more than you think.
items.summary: For a quarter century, the Google search box has been one of the most recognizable interfaces in computing: a thin white rectangle, a blinking cursor, a few typed words, and a list of blue links. On Tuesday, Google will formally retire that paradigm. At its annual I/O developer c

items.headline: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
items.summary: Railway, a San Francisco-based cloud platform that has quietly amassed two million developers without spending a dollar on marketing, announced Thursday that it raised $100 million in a Series B funding round, as surging demand for artificial intelligence applications exposes the

items.headline: Advancing next-gen AI with materials science innovation
items.summary: The conversation about AI often centers on algorithms, computing power, or huge investments in new semiconductor fabrication plants and hyperscale data centers. But beneath each of these advances is another layer of innovation that makes them possible: advanced materials. Every n

items.summary: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. Over the weekend, several current and former advisors to President Donald Trump on AI publicly lobbed insults at the country’s leading AI comp

items.headline: AI is more likely than humans to form biases when hiring
items.summary: The next time you apply for a job, AI may screen your résumé before any human sees it. But there’s good reason to question whether AI will judge you fairly. Researchers already know that LLMs pick up human biases from their training data. New research suggests that LLMs can also

items.headline: The risk of weather data sabotage is rising
items.summary: Every morning, airline dispatchers, grid operators, and farmers around the world make decisions based on the same thing: a weather forecast. While these forecasts are something that most people glance at for two seconds, weather predictions influence major strategic decisions in

items.headline: Meet GPT-Red: an LLM super-hacker OpenAI built to make its models safer
items.summary: OpenAI has built an LLM super-hacker called GPT-Red that it uses as a sparring partner to help its other models boost their defenses against cyberattacks. Last week the company released the latest version of its flagship LLM, GPT-5.6. OpenAI says that training it against GPT-Red

items.headline: What Anthropic’s latest AI discovery does—and doesn’t—show
items.summary: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. Anthropic—currently the world’s most valuable AI company, with a nearly $1 trillion valuation—has a reputation for publishing strange and head

items.headline: Anthropic found a hidden space where Claude puzzles over concepts
items.summary: The AI firm Anthropic has developed a technique that has given it the clearest glimpse yet at what’s really going on inside large language models as they answer questions or carry out tasks. What they found ranges from the mundane to the unnerving. Researchers at the company buil

items.headline: The foundational elements of AI architecture that IT leaders need to scale
items.summary: With the rapid progress of AI capabilities and the move to agentic systems, organizations are expanding their use cases as the technology continues to grow. That constant evolution also introduces risk, leaving IT leaders to wonder which investments will prove valuable even six m

items.headline: A Sneaky Hacking Tool Targeting AI Infrastructure Is Lurking in Victims’ Blind Spots
items.summary: A new type of malware can worm deep into AI coding systems to steal data and logins—and can flip a “death switch” to destroy files and keep out real users.

items.headline: Nvidia Wants to Own Every Chip Inside AI Data Centers
items.summary: Nvidia’s Vera Rubin platform combines CPUs and GPUs into a single system, reflecting the company’s growing ambition to power every layer of AI infrastructure.

items.headline: Halliday’s New Smart Glasses Skip the Camera
items.summary: Halliday’s G2 glasses can listen to and summarize your workplace meetings—with no video involved.

items.headline: ‘It’s a Modern-Day Draft’: Why Stanford Students Walked Out on Sundar Pichai’s Commencement Speech
items.summary: Last month, more than a hundred Stanford students left their own graduation to protest Google’s military contracts and deals with ICE. Two organizers, Amanda Campos and Eva Jones, tell us why.

items.headline: The Army Is Burning Through Its AI Tokens
items.summary: Members of the Army received an email informing them that they were rapidly depleting their AI tokens, and needed to limit use.

items.headline: Etsy Is In Its Flop Era, and Sellers Are Fleeing
items.summary: Once a quirky bastion of amateur vulva jewelry and pet portraits, Etsy is now deluged with mass-produced goods and AI knockoffs. Some customers don’t seem to mind.

items.headline: Your Period Tracker Is (Probably) Spying on You
items.summary: Plus: Russian cyberspies turn to infrastructure hacking, DHS repeatedly fails to realize it’d been hacked, a breach exposes an AI music generator’s scraping ways, and more.

items.summary: Now that Google has changed how its usage quotas are tallied, you might not get as many AI responses as you did before.
